LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF RED EAGLE AIR CORP., A UTAH CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. AS AS MATTER OF LAW, THE LIQUIDATED DAMAGES PROVISION WAS NOT REASONABLE AT THE TIME THE LEASE WAS SIGNED BECAUSE: A) THE AMOUNT OF LIQUIDATED DAMAGES DOES NOT BEAR ANY REASONABLE RELATIONSHIP TO THE AMOUNT OF THE LIKELY ACTUAL DAMAGES; B) THE AMOUNT OF LIQUIDATED DAMAGES HAS AN INVERSE RELATIONSHIP TO THE LIKELY ACTUAL DAMAGES INSOFAR AS THE TIMING OF THE BREACH IS CONCERNED (I.E. THE EARLIER THE BREACH AND THE LESS LIKELY THE AMOUNT OF THE ACTUAL DAMAGES, THE PROVISION IMPOSES A GREATER AMOUNT OF LIQUIDATED DAMAGES AND VICE-VERSA) AND C) THE PROVISION VITIATES THE IMPORTANT AND LONGSTANDING PRINCIPLE OF CALIFORNIA LAW THAT THE NON-BREACHING PARTY HAS A DUTY TO MITIGATE ITS DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-06-2012 Vacated

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Feb-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court vacated its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MR AVIATION, LLC'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is taken off calendar at the request of the moving party. (Dismissal filed.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Deborah Neville, CSR 703

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KEVIN R. BRODEHL OF WENDEL & ROSEN, TEL. NO. (510) 622-7530 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KENNETH C. WHITE OF BLOOSKY INTERACTIVE, LLC, TEL. NO. 1-888-637-7220 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT MR. AVIATION INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF RED EAGLE AIR CORP., A UTAH CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. As as matter of law, the liquidated damages provision was not reasonable at the time the lease was signed because: a) the amount of liquidated damages does not bear any reasonable relationship to the amount of the likely actual damages; b) the amount of liquidated damages has an inverse relationship to the likely actual damages insofar as the timing of the breach is concerned (i.e. the earlier the breach and the less likely the amount of the actual damages, the provision imposes a greater amount of liquidated damages and vice-versa) and c) the provision vitiates the important and longstanding principle of California law that the non-breaching party has a duty to mitigate its damages. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: La Vena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (302/HEK)
